_comment: Strip unverified eatery names from blog prose — same rule as stays. Where the name is load-bearing (food-trail blogs), replace with a pattern-pointer to the destination's eats section. Generated 2026-04-22 from scripts/audit-blog-eateries.mjs.

rewrites.find: Jimmy's Italian Kitchen: Wood-fired pizza that has no business being this good at 2,000m.
rewrites.replace: One unassuming Italian kitchen in upper Mcleodganj turns out wood-fired pizza that has no business being this good at 2,000m — the local food scene's open secret.

rewrites.find: Common Ground Cafe: Cinnamon rolls and drip coffee.
rewrites.replace: A low-key cafe off the main drag does cinnamon rolls and drip coffee.

rewrites.find: Eat at: Rajdhani (Gujarati thali, unlimited food, ₹600), Wenger's (bakery since 1926), or Farzi Cafe (modern Indian, ₹1,500 per person).
rewrites.replace: Eat at: a veg-thali chain (Rajdhani or Govinda's, unlimited food around ₹600), Wenger's (Delhi institution bakery, since 1926), or any modern-Indian place in Connaught Place (₹1,200-1,800 per person).

rewrites.find: **Cafes on the Main Bazaar strip** (Honey & Spice, Sunset Cafe, the rooftop at Seventh Heaven) are running at 60% capacity in October and 130% in November.
rewrites.replace: **Cafes on the Main Bazaar strip** are running at 60% capacity in October and 130% in November — the rooftop cafes with ghat views fill first.

rewrites.find: Gypsy Restaurant on Airport Road for full Marwari thali: ker sangri, gatte ki sabzi, bajra roti.
rewrites.replace: A full Marwari thali on Airport Road (ker sangri, gatte ki sabzi, bajra roti) is the classic Jodhpur plate — Jodhpur locals can point you to the current best.

rewrites.find: Rajasthan is predominantly vegetarian, but Handi Restaurant in Jaipur does a credible laal maas.
rewrites.replace: Rajasthan is predominantly vegetarian, but a handful of Jaipur restaurants do a credible laal maas — ask at your hotel for the current pick.

rewrites.find: **Women-owned stays**: Seven Hills Cafe & Guesthouse, Trimurti Garden Cafe, Bhagsu Cafe guesthouses.
rewrites.replace: **Women-owned stays**: a cluster of small cafe-guesthouses in the Bhagsu neighbourhood — see the [Mcleodganj stays section](/en/destination/mcleodganj) for vetted picks.

rewrites.find: New Year parties worth doing: Sunburn Festival (when running — check dates) is the big commercial option; Café Mambo, Tito's, LPK are mainstream;
rewrites.replace: New Year parties worth doing: Sunburn Festival (when running — check dates) is the big commercial option; the Baga strip clubs (Tito's Lane) are mainstream;

rewrites.find: Shillong's food scene is underrated: Trattoria (local Khasi), Cafe Shillong Heritage, ML 05 Cafe.
rewrites.replace: Shillong's food scene is underrated — Khasi, Chinese, Italian, and cafe-culture pockets all within a 2km radius. See the [Shillong eats section](/en/destination/shillong) for vetted picks.

rewrites.find: Afternoon: Tibetan cooking class (book through your hotel or search "Lhamo's Kitchen" — ₹1,500 per person, 3 hours).
rewrites.replace: Afternoon: Tibetan cooking class (book through your Dharamshala hotel — ₹1,500 per person, 3 hours; multiple operators run them).
